scope.summary: Implement automatic joint-mapping from imported FBX/glTF skeleton hierarchies (Daz 3D, Mixamo, MetaHuman, Character Creator naming conventions) to the canonical Tailor joint name set used by CollisionCapsule.joint_name, writing a normalized ClothBodyProxy from the imported rig
acceptance_criteria: A JointMapper type accepts a flat joint-name list from an imported skeleton and outputs a BTreeMap<TailorJointName, ImportedJointName> using heuristic name matching plus a configurable override map

At least four naming conventions are auto-resolved without override: Mixamo (mixamorig:Hips, mixamorig:LeftArm...), Daz Genesis (hip, lThighBend...), UE5 Mannequin (pelvis, thigh_l...), and standard VRM (J_Bip_C_Hips...)
Un-mappable joints emit a JointMappingWarning event (not an error) and are excluded from capsule generation
The resulting capsule chain covers all mapped joint pairs and produces a valid ClothBodyProxy (no interpenetrating capsules, all radii > 0)
TailorAvatarCreated + TailorBodyProxyCreated are emitted on successful import with a source_kind tag of the detected convention
cargo test joint_mapper::test_mixamo_auto_map passes with >= 22 of 25 joints correctly resolved
scope.risk_if_missed: Every non-default avatar import requires manual capsule authoring. Artists using Daz, Mixamo, or MetaHuman cannot onboard characters in a self-service workflow, making custom avatar support practically unusable for the majority of digital-character pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
